---
applyTo: "**/*.md"
---

# 📝 Markdown Documentation Guidelines

## Purpose & Scope

These guidelines ensure documentation in HomeRacker stays consistent, discoverable, and actionable.
Every code change that adds, modifies, or removes functionality **must** include a corresponding documentation update.

## ✍️ Writing Style

- Use **emojis** to improve scannability (section headers, admonitions, list bullets where fitting).
- Be **brief** — prefer bullet points over prose. Link to existing docs instead of duplicating content.
- Use **code blocks** with language hints for commands and config snippets.
- Cross-reference from the parent `README.md` when adding new pages so they remain discoverable.

## 📐 Document Structure

If not stated otherwise by the user, every new documentation page must follow this skeleton:

```markdown
# 🏷️ Title

## 📌 What

What component/feature/process this covers and which code paths are affected.

## 🤔 Why

Use-case description, design rationale, and/or architectural context.

## 🔧 How

Step-by-step instructions, commands, and/or examples for a user and/or CI to integrate/execute.

## 📚 References

- Originating issue / PR / discussion
- Related documentation in the repo
- Upstream / vendor documentation
```

> Sections may be omitted only when truly not applicable (e.g., a pure reference page).

## 🏗️ Model README Template

Each model folder (`models/<name>/`) should have a `README.md` following this structure:

```markdown
# 🏷️ Model Name

## 📌 What

Brief description of the model — what it is, what problem it solves.

## 🤔 Why

Design rationale: why this shape, these dimensions, this approach.

## 🔧 How

How to use/customize the model:
- Customizer parameters
- OpenSCAD CLI examples
- Print settings or tips (if applicable)

## 📸 Catalog

Preview images for each part. Generate with `scadm export-png`:

| Part | Preview |
|------|---------|
| Part Name | ![Part Name](parts/part_name.png) |

> For flat models (no `parts/` subfolder), show a single preview next to the `.scad` file.

To generate or refresh previews:

  scadm export-png models/<name>/parts/<part>.scad

## 📚 References

- Related models or libraries
- MakerWorld listing (if published)
```

> - The 📸 Catalog section must list **every** `.scad` file under `parts/` (or the model's main `.scad` for flat models) with a preview PNG.
> - PNGs are generated by `scadm export-png` and stored next to their source `.scad` file.
> - When adding or modifying a parts file, **always** generate/refresh its PNG and update the Catalog table.
> - When adding a new model or part, also update the parent `models/README.md` index with a description and preview image.

## 🗂️ File Organization

- **Placement:** READMEs live next to their code — each model folder and `cmd/` subfolder gets its own `README.md`.
- **Naming:** Use lowercase `kebab-case`. Name files after the topic, not the ticket.
- **Restructuring:** When adding docs, check if existing files should be renamed or reorganized to avoid overlap. Keep the directory tree tidy — fewer well-named files beat many scattered ones.
- **Index:** Reference new model folders from `models/README.md`.

## 🔗 Linking Conventions

- Use **relative paths** for repo-internal links.
- For external resources (BOSL2 wiki, MakerWorld), include the full URL.

## 🚫 Anti-Patterns

- Don't create docs that only repeat what the code already says — add *context* the code can't convey.
- Don't leave placeholder sections empty — either fill them or remove the heading.
- Don't duplicate content across files — link to the single source of truth.
- Don't commit PNGs without a corresponding README entry linking them.
- Don't add or modify parts files without generating/refreshing preview PNGs.
- Don't update a model README without checking if the parent `models/README.md` index needs updating too.
